name: GlobalLogic
description: Two stacks of metallic cards animated with a custom diagonal-bend vertex shader. The cards flex in a synchronized wave while the camera orbits with variable speed. Dramatic orange and red point lights create depth against a white fog background.
reason: This project was created for GlobalLogic to showcase their brand in a visually engaging way instead of a static image.

name: N-IX Branding Live Background
description: A visualization of 1,000 metallic spheres (InstancedMesh) that morph between seven geometric shapes — circle, cube, sphere, Perlin-noise terrain, and DNA helix. Features a live procedural background shader with an animated studio hotspot, orbiting colored lights, per-sphere blinking, and full GUI controls for animation, material, and camera.
reason: On the N-IX website, was used 512 images in the dom! To make animation more efficient, I created a live background with 1,000 spheres that morph between seven geometric shapes. The background is procedurally generated and animated in real-time.

name: Live Background for any Card
description: A full landing page where a single fixed WebGL canvas renders live backgrounds inside every card simultaneously using scissor testing — one draw call per card, zero DOM canvases. Each card gets its own coordinate space via uBlockOffset. The shader layers three effects: five slowly drifting bokeh blobs in cyan, blue, and purple; a grid of twinkling static sparkles with per-cell random phase and speed; and 240 perspective-projected flying stars that cycle depth with fract() to create a continuous hyperspace zoom effect.
reason: A proof of concept for rendering rich animated backgrounds across an entire page with a single WebGL context — instead of one canvas per card, scissor testing lets the GPU clip the same shader to each card's bounding rect, keeping memory and overhead minimal.

name: GameInspire Tunnel Grid
description: Two curved dot-grid planes — one above and one below the camera — rendered as WebGL point sprites and scrolled toward the viewer in a continuous tunnel. A cylindrical bend compresses the X axis and a per-plane Y curvature arches the grids toward each other. The vertex shader handles perspective projection, near/far/edge alpha falloff, and a slow view-axis rotation. Top dots are cool blue, bottom dots are soft purple, both dimming as they approach the camera.
reason: Created for GameInspire to provide an immersive live background for their platform — a subtle infinite-tunnel animation in blue and purple tones that conveys depth and forward momentum without distracting from the content. On the site this element is a static image.

name: theMultiple Slot Drums
description: Three spinning slot-machine drums rendered entirely via ray marching signed distance functions — no geometry buffers. Each cylinder hosts six icon panels read from an SVG atlas, shaded with diffuse/specular lighting and glowing cyan torus rings that levitate around each drum. A fish-eye camera and a 10-second animation cycle featuring cubic ease-out deceleration snapping to sector boundaries give the piece a polished, tactile feel.
reason: Created for theMultiple to showcase their brand through an interactive slot-machine metaphor, combining custom SVG logos with geometric symbols on procedurally rendered 3D drums. They have animation on site but it based on image sequence. I created a real-time 3D version with ray marching and signed distance functions.
